The information included in a network engineer job description usually contains a list of specific duties and responsibilities and mentions the required qualifications and skills for the role. Employers typically post their preferences and requirements in the job description to help attract matching and suitable candidates. This incentivises candidates to prepare their applications accordingly to showcase their potential fit, which can result in a more efficient hiring process.
The job description may also indicate details about the work environment such as the nature of the business and the type of data you can work with. Depending on the employer's requirements, network engineers may be stationed at an office or travel to work sites to provide support when necessary. A network engineer typically designs, installs, configures and maintains computer networks.
They also troubleshoot any issues and ensure that the network is secure and efficient. Network engineers frequently collaborate with other information technology (IT) team members to develop network policies and stay up-to-date with emerging technologies to ensure the network remains secure and scalable.

Job Title: Assistant Manager/ Manager, Counter Unmanned Aircraft Systems
Job Description: Company description: Changi Airport Group (CAG) is the manager of Singapore Changi Airport, a leading air hub in Asia and one of the world's most awarded airports.  As airport manager, CAG performs the key functions of airport operations, air hub development, retail and commercial activities, infrastructure development and airport emergency services. CAG also manages Seletar Airport, and through its subsidiary, Changi Airports International, it takes Changi's presence beyond Singapore's shores through consultancy projects and investments in foreign airports.  Come join us today! Job description:As a key member of our Counter-UAS team, you will collaborate closely with governmental bodies, external partners, technical experts, vendors, and internal stakeholders to oversee the project management, operation, and maintenance of our cutting-edge C-UAS systems deployed at Singapore Changi Airport. Your responsibilities will span from tender evaluation and system installation to testing, commissioning, and handover, ensuring seamless project implementation.In this role, you will have the opportunity to contribute to policy development, enhance operating procedures, and strategize maintenance initiatives to optimize system availability. Your efforts will directly contribute to fortifying Changi's aviation space, mitigating potential risks to aircraft safety, and ensuring uninterrupted flight operations. Additionally, you will engage in collaborative efforts with regulatory authorities, vendors, OEMs, and maintenance contractors to uphold system reliability and meet stringent regulatory standards. Your involvement in continuous improvement projects will further bolster our core competencies and enhance the capabilities of our C-UAS systems. Responsibilities:  Lead project management and maintenance activities for Counter-UAS systems at Changi Airport.   Assist in procurement tender evaluation, system installation, testing, and commissioning.   Foster technical capability and knowledge development within the Counter-UAS team.   Drive initiatives to enhance system planning, development, and implementation.   Ensure system reliability, safety, and cost-effectiveness throughout project lifecycle.   Spearhead digitization and innovation efforts within the C-UAS domain.   Facilitate technical discussions and collaborate with internal and external stakeholders.   Oversee vendor management to uphold system performance standards.   Conduct safety assessment reviews for new system deployments.   Requirements:  Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Computing, Telecommunication, or equivalent.   Proficiency in drone detection sensors, effector technologies, and frequency spectrum communication.   Preferably 3 years of experience in Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) and Counter-UAS systems.   Aviation industry experience is a plus.   Ability to remain calm and resolve unforeseen challenges.   Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.   Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication abilities.   Proficient in project management, collaboration, and coordination.   Demonstrated presentation skills.   Willingness to perform night duties as required for project activities.  If you're ready to make a significant impact in aviation security and advance your career in a dynamic environment, we invite you to be part of our innovative Counter-UAS team!
